description_long: This is a small, single-story brick house with a prominent gable roof covered in tan shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from reddish-brown bricks, and a large rectangular chimney rises along one side of the house. The front facade features a simple wooden door accessed by a short set of steps, alongside a single rectangular window. The rear and side elevations each have one window, with the window placements being asymmetrical. The house sits on a dark, slightly raised foundation, and the overall design is modest and functional, typical of early to mid-20th-century American residential architecture.
description: A compact, single-story brick house with a gable shingle roof, chimney, wooden door, and asymmetrical windows.
description_view_0: The side view shows the full height of the brick chimney centered on the wall, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_1: The front view displays the main entryway with a wooden door, steps, and a single window to the right of the door.
description_view_2: The opposite side view features a single window and the chimney visible at the roof's peak.
description_view_3: The rear view shows two windows of different sizes, with no door or chimney visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door with a small set of steps, no porch or overhang, minimal decorative elements.
